title: Autonomous zero-brief frontend build workflow
applies_to: minimal website or page creation prompt
inputs: current repository and running product when available
supplied facts, names, quoted text, assets, and constraints

steps.title: Inspect the project and classify the build
actions: Inspect framework, routes, assets, dependencies, design-system conventions, and the running product when available.
Choose new-build or redesign without replacing useful architecture.
Extract named people, quoted text, products, groups, events, and concepts from the request.
exit_criteria: The operating mode is autonomous-zero-brief-build and project constraints are explicit.

steps.title: Infer a reversible creative brief
actions: Infer domain, product type, interface archetype, purpose, primary and secondary tasks, audience, message, emotional objective, trust, risk, density, frequency, seriousness, maturity, devices, accessibility needs, visual and motion intensity, experimental tolerance, familiarity, page type, and required states.
Separate supplied facts from creative assumptions.
Continue without routine questions when uncertainty is reversible.
exit_criteria: The brief is specific enough to reject generic or contextually wrong output.

steps.title: Choose direction and write complete copy
actions: Write a one-sentence design thesis, derive two or three useful signals from the subject's materials, tools, language, environment, or evidence, and select a context-specific intensity, composition, hero treatment, type, color/material, imagery, component, and motion stance; when the stance is non-static, name a compact motion grammar rather than leaving motion as a final flourish.
Treat quality adjectives as execution targets rather than automatic permission for flashy, dark, cinematic, or highly animated styling.
Retrieve only brief-stage product, content, direction, composition, typography, responsive, accessibility, integrity, and completion records.
Write original finished copy without placeholders or unsupported factual claims.
Keep the smallest copy set that preserves the task, decision, trust, and recovery information; remove repeated messages and sections with no distinct job.
exit_criteria: Direction and copy support the inferred purpose and differ meaningfully across different prompts.

steps.title: Implement the complete frontend
actions: Implement semantic structure, responsive composition, finished copy, functional controls, and reachable states.
Add keyboard behavior, visible focus, accessible names, error recovery, and reduced-motion behavior during implementation.

For a non-static direction, implement a compact motion grammar across focal or narrative beats, meaningful state changes, and direct interaction feedback; keep ordinary reading content stable and preserve equivalent reduced-motion outcomes.
exit_criteria: The primary experience is complete and honest rather than a scaffold, plan, or wireframe.

steps.title: Run and inspect rendered desktop and mobile output
actions: Run the application and capture meaningful desktop and mobile screenshots.

Wait for an explicit user-visible ready state, inspect the rendered roles, labels, DOM, screenshot, and console before selecting interaction targets, then inspect hierarchy, composition, typography, spacing, color, imagery, copy, motion, responsive behavior, focus, and overflow.
Compare the rendered result with the design thesis and record the three highest-impact weaknesses.
exit_criteria: The implementation was visually inspected at both desktop and mobile widths; static analysis alone is insufficient.

steps.title: Replace generic output and fix the largest gaps
actions: Run the contextual anti-slop review and require a product or narrative reason for familiar generated patterns, including empty scale masquerading as minimalism.
Fix the three highest-impact weaknesses.
Capture and inspect the revised interface again.
exit_criteria: Remaining changes are low value or a real limitation is documented.

steps.title: Verify production completion and report concisely
actions: Run the production build and applicable type, lint, test, accessibility, link, metadata, console, overflow, and configured private-term checks.
Verify routes and asset paths from production output.
Report the result, evidence, assumptions, limitations, and deployment boundary without narrating routine process.
exit_criteria: Every applicable blocking gate passes or is reported honestly with reproducible evidence.
